briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 23, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Widespread Impacts
sections.content: Climate change is having widespread and severe impacts on human health and ecosystems. Domestically, climate change is leading to an increase not only in heatstroke and infectious diseases but also allergic conditions, and warnings about mental health in the era of climate crisis have also been raised [2, 6]. Furthermore, the climate crisis is causing difficulties for the oyster farming industry, making the search for solutions urgent [10]. Internationally, concerns about climate change among U.S. citizens have reached near-record levels, and environmental destruction is accelerating, with significant losses of soil inorganic carbon being confirmed [61, 62]. In Idaho, USA, severe drought and lack of snowfall are being linked to climate change, and warnings continue that wildfires will hit homeless populations and have a tangible impact on the quality of life for Los Angeles residents [68, 71, 72, 75].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Expansion of Domestic Carbon Neutrality Practices and Strengthening Education
sections.content: Amidst this crisis, diverse efforts to implement carbon neutrality are actively underway domestically. Western Power promoted its carbon reduction achievements during the '2026 K-GX Climate Change Week,' and various local governments and organizations, including Cheongdo-gun, Ansan Green Environment Center, and Hapcheon-gun, conducted carbon neutrality practice campaigns and environmental cleanup activities to commemorate Earth Day [1, 4, 7, 9, 12, 19, 25, 26, 27, 28, 32, 33, 36]. Notably, Anyang City is conducting carbon-neutral green growth education, and the Future Carbon Neutrality Forum is fostering experts by producing 42 carbon neutrality instructors [13, 14, 16, 17, 34]. Furthermore, Asan Baebang-eup has signed carbon neutrality practice agreements with daycare centers, and the Yesan-gun Saemaul Association is encouraging citizen participation through companion plant sharing, thereby spreading a carbon-neutral culture throughout the local community [20, 21]. Siheung City is strengthening its carbon-neutral city efforts by establishing an environmental education plan, and Gyeongbuk Culture and Tourism Corporation is transforming the Bomun Golf Club into a carbon-neutral golf course, creating tangible changes in various sectors [29, 31].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Technological Innovation
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy and technological innovation, key drivers in responding to climate change, are also accelerating. KOICA is exploring ways to assist developing countries in operating climate change response technologies using Artificial Intelligence (AI), opening new horizons for technological cooperation [3]. Domestically, K-Shipping has taken its first step in ammonia bunkering at Ulsan Port, striving to secure a leading position in the carbon-neutral market, and as demand for renewable energy increased due to soaring oil prices, IKEA began selling solar panels [35, 39]. NextEra recorded its largest-ever backlog of renewable energy orders, and Gyeongnam Energy signed an agreement with Tongyeong City to promote renewable energy projects, indicating expanding investment and participation from the industry [42, 43]. The achievements of building-integrated photovoltaic (BIPV) system research projects are being shared, and China's solar power strategy is changing the energy landscape in Southeast Asia, demonstrating active dissemination and expansion of renewable energy technologies internationally [46, 48, 49].

sections.title: 🌍 Policy Challenges and the Mixed Results of International Climate Action
sections.content: Policy efforts and international cooperation to address climate change are ongoing, but they are simultaneously facing several challenges. Jeollanam-do hosted the 3rd UN Climate Change Convention at the Yeosu World Expo site, providing a venue for international discussions [5]. However, domestically, civil society organizations are warning that carbon neutrality is impossible without renewable energy, and criticisms are being raised about the insufficient implementation of provincial carbon neutrality policies, revealing gaps in policy execution [18, 22]. Internationally, movements counter to climate action have been observed in some countries, such as the Florida governor signing a bill to repeal local climate change measures and the exclusion of climate change agenda from G7 meetings [56, 63, 76]. Meanwhile, in the U.S., legislation is being advanced for the EPA to review the impacts of climate change on Superfund sites, and the EU is exploring new policy responses, such as announcing a strategy to limit fossil fuel price shocks [60, 79].

briefing.closing: Through today's briefing, we have been able to confirm the severity of the climate crisis, along with various domestic and international efforts to overcome it. This is a time when stronger and more consistent climate action is required for a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Legendary Masterpiece, Popular on OTT, Returns to Theaters After 18 Years: 'The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas'
articles.original_title: OTT 떠돌던 전설의 명화, 18년 만에 극장 나들이···‘줄무늬 파자마를 입은 소년’
articles.description: The pure friendship between a German and a Jewish boy dramatically reveals the horrors of the Holocaust. A classic beloved even without theatrical release, it returns amidst a deepening 'new release drought' post-COVID-19. Distributors are opting for proven re-releases, offering nostalgia for older audiences and novelty for younger viewers, though differentiation remains a challenge. (Image: Two boys from the film look at each other across a barbed wire fence. Provided by Pop Entertainment.)

summary.phenomenon: The film 'The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas,' beloved on OTT platforms, is being re-released in theaters after 18 years. This occurs amidst a deepening drought of new releases following the COVID-19 pandemic.
summary.cause: Following the COVID-19 pandemic, the deepening scarcity of new releases in cinemas has led distributors to increasingly select proven re-releases.
summary.outlook: While re-releases offer nostalgia for middle-aged audiences and novelty for those in their 20s, they present a dilemma: low risk but difficulty in achieving differentiation.

articles.original_description: 독일·유대인 소년의 순수한 우정홀로코스트 참상 극적으로 드러내극장 상영 없이도 사랑받은 ‘고전’코로나19 이후 ‘신작 가뭄’ 심화배급사들, 검증된 재개봉작 선택중장년층엔 향수, 20대엔 새로움 위험 적지만 차별화 어려워 ‘고심’영화 의 두 소년이 철조망을 사이에 두고 서로를 바라보고 있다. 팝엔터테인먼트 제공영화를 보···

articles.title: [Exclusive] 123 'Sunshine' Cooperatives Mushroom in Three Months... Residents Pushed Out, Feared to Become a 'Money Feast for Outsiders'
articles.original_title: [단독]석 달 새 ‘햇빛’ 협동조합 123개 우후죽순···주민 밀려나고 ‘외지인 돈잔치’ 될라
articles.description: Over 120 'Sunshine' cooperatives, established to participate in the government-promoted 'Sunshine Income Village' project, have rapidly increased this year. Concerns are rising that external businesses, such as solar power generation companies or consulting firms, may have hastily formed these cooperatives, potentially causing actual project stakeholders, the village residents, to lose their standing.

summary.phenomenon: In the first three months of this year, over 120 'Sunshine' cooperatives, formed to participate in the government's 'Sunshine Income Village' project, have rapidly increased.
summary.cause: It is highly probable that external businesses, such as solar power generation companies or consulting firms, have hastily formed these cooperatives.
summary.outlook: As a result, concerns are being raised that the actual project stakeholders, the village residents, may lose their standing.

articles.original_description: 경기 파주시 적성면의 논에 태양광 판넬이 설치돼 있다. 권도현 기자정부가 추진하는 햇빛소득마을 사업 참여를 위한 ‘햇빛’ 협동조합이 올해 120개 넘게 급증한 것으로 확인됐다. 태양광 발전 관련 기업이나 컨설팅 업체 등 외부 사업자들이 협동조합을 급조했을 가능성이 높아, 정작 사업 주체인 마을 주민이 설 자리를 잃을 수 있다는 우려가 나온다.3월 한 달간 ···

articles.title: This Masterpiece Film, Wandering on OTT, Gets Its First Domestic Theatrical Release? Coming to Korea After 18 Years
articles.original_title: OTT 떠돌던 이 명작 영화, 국내 첫 개봉이라고?···18년 만에 한국 온다
articles.description: The film 'The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas,' a masterpiece depicting the horrors of the Holocaust, is set for its first theatrical release in Korea. Despite being consistently loved through word-of-mouth without prior theatrical runs, its release comes amid a deepening 'new release drought' post-COVID-19, leading distributors to opt for proven re-releases. It is expected to evoke nostalgia for older generations and offer a novel experience for those in their 20s, presenting a low-risk, differentiated strategy for distributors.

summary.phenomenon: The film 'The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas' is set for its first theatrical release in Korea after 18 years.
summary.cause: As new film releases decreased after the COVID-19 pandemic, the tendency for distributors to choose re-released films with proven box office success has intensified.
summary.outlook: This film is expected to provide nostalgia for middle-aged audiences and a new experience for those in their 20s, serving as a low-risk, differentiated strategy for distributors.

articles.original_description: 영화 ‘줄무늬 파자마를 입은 소년’ 국내 최초 개봉영화 의 두 소년이 철조망을 사이에 두고 서로를 바라보고 있다. 팝엔터테인먼트 제공홀로코스트 참상 담은 명작 입소문극장 상영 없이도 꾸준히 사랑받아코로나19 이후 ‘신작 가뭄’ 심화배급사들, 검증된 재개봉작 선택중장년층엔 향수, 20대엔 새로움위험 적지만 차별화 어···

articles.title: Hyundai Motor Records 'Record-High Revenue' in Q1, Yet Operating Profit Drops by 30%
articles.original_title: 1분기 ‘최대 매출’ 찍고도…현대차, 영업익 30% 감소
articles.description: Hyundai Motor's operating profit for the first quarter of this year decreased by over 30% compared to the same period last year. Despite achieving record-high revenue for a first quarter, the company was unable to overcome the challenges posed by US tariffs and the Middle East situation. To address sluggish sales in the Chinese market, Hyundai Motor is transitioning to an EV-centric eco-friendly car brand, 24 years after its initial entry.

summary.phenomenon: Despite Hyundai Motor recording its highest-ever revenue in the first quarter of this year, its operating profit decreased by over 30% compared to the same period last year.
summary.cause: This decrease in operating profit is attributed to the impact of US tariffs and the situation in the Middle East.
summary.outlook: To overcome sluggishness in the Chinese market, Hyundai Motor is pursuing a strategy to transition to an EV-centric eco-friendly car brand, 24 years after its entry into the local market.

articles.original_description: 미 관세·중동 사태 파고 못 넘어글로벌 판매 대수 2.5% 줄었지만하이브리드 등 친환경차 ‘선전’현대자동차의 올해 1분기 영업이익이 전년 동기 대비 30% 이상 감소했다. 1분기 기준 역대 최대 매출 기록을 세우고도 미국 관세와 중동 사태 파고를 넘지 못했다. 현대차는 중국 시장 부진을 타개하기 위해 현지 진출 24년 만에 전기차 중심의 친환경차 브랜드로 ···

articles.original_title: Republican lawmakers attempt to shield big oil from climate lawsuits in ‘alarming’ bills
articles.description: Climate experts and advocates warn House and Senate bills will protect polluters at the cost of the climate Republican lawmakers are attempting to shield big oil from having to pay for its contributions to the climate crisis, alarming environmental advocates.New House and Senate bills, led by Harriet Hageman, a Wyoming representative, and Ted Cruz, a Texas senator, respectively, would give oil and gas companies broad legal immunity from policies and lawsuits aimed at holding the industry account

summary.phenomenon: Republican lawmakers are attempting to grant broad legal immunity to major oil and gas companies through House and Senate bills, aiming to shield them from accountability for their contributions to the climate crisis.
summary.outlook: Climate experts and advocates are warning that these bills will protect polluters at the expense of the climate.

articles.original_description: Climate experts and advocates warn House and Senate bills will protect polluters at the cost of the climate Republican lawmakers are attempting to shield big oil from having to pay for its contributions to the climate crisis, alarming environmental advocates.New House and Senate bills, led by Harriet Hageman, a Wyoming representative, and Ted Cruz, a Texas senator, respectively, would give oil and gas companies broad legal immunity from policies and lawsuits aimed at holding the industry account
